steps.title: Apply the triangle area formula
steps.content: The area of a triangle depends on its base and height. We have base = 48 cm and height = 96 cm.

steps.title: Substitute and calculate the area
steps.content:
Substitute base = 48 and height = 96 into the formula:
$$A = \frac{1}{2} \cdot 48 \cdot 96$$
Simplify by calculating $\frac{1}{2} \cdot 48 = 24$:
$$A = 24 \cdot 96 = 2,304$$
The area is 2,304 square centimeters. That matches choice A — A is correct.
